title: US federal AI enforcement tracker
description: Federal AI enforcement actions from the FTC, SEC, DOJ, EEOC, and FCC with dates, penalties, docket numbers, legal authorities, and primary sources. Covers AI washing, algorithmic pricing, AI hiring discrimination, deepfake robocalls, and AI-company investor fraud. Not legal advice; confirm against the linked primary sources.
citation: Vorp Labs, US Federal AI Enforcement Tracker (as of 2026-07-10), https://vorplabs.com/ai-regulatory-updates/federal-enforcement

actions.statusDetail: Stipulated order filed 2024-03-08; FTC case page shows Pending but order is in effect.
actions.outcome: Five-year ban on facial recognition for security/surveillance; biometric safeguards and information-security obligations.
actions.summary: FTC alleged Rite Aid deployed AI facial-recognition surveillance without reasonable safeguards, misidentifying consumers and disproportionately impacting people of color. The stipulated order bans Rite Aid from using facial recognition or analysis systems for security or surveillance for five years and imposes biometric safeguards.
actions.aiIssue: AI facial-recognition surveillance / biometric automated systems

factChecks.label: Order status
factChecks.value: Stipulated order filed 2024-03-08 in E.D. Pa.; FTC case page lists Matter/File Number 2023190.

factChecks.label: Remedy
factChecks.value: Rite Aid is prohibited from using facial recognition technology for security or surveillance purposes for five years.

actions.target: NGL Labs / Raj Vir / Joao Figueiredo
actions.statusDetail: Refund claims intake closed 2026-04-06; payments pending distribution. FTC case page shows Pending.
actions.outcome: $4.5M FTC redress plus $500k Los Angeles DA civil penalty; ban on offering anonymous messaging apps to under-18 users; AI-moderation misrepresentation ban.
actions.summary: FTC and Los Angeles DA alleged NGL Labs falsely claimed its AI content-moderation program filtered out cyberbullying and other harmful messages, while the app sent fake messages designed to lure users into paid subscriptions and marketed to children.

factChecks.label: Penalty
factChecks.value: Proposed order included $4.5 million redress and $500,000 civil penalty to the Los Angeles DA's office.

factChecks.label: Claims status
factChecks.value: FTC refund claims intake closed 2026-04-06; payments pending distribution.

openItems: FTC matter/file number and court docket not verified from public materials.

actions.statusDetail: Final order issued February 2025.
actions.outcome: Bars deceptive AI-lawyer claims; monetary relief and notice to past subscribers.
actions.summary: FTC alleged DoNotPay deceptively marketed itself as 'the world's first robot lawyer,' claiming its AI could substitute for a human lawyer when the product could not.

factChecks.label: Final order
factChecks.value: FTC finalized order against DoNotPay in February 2025, prohibiting deceptive AI lawyer claims and imposing monetary relief.

openItems: FTC matter/file number not verified from public materials.

actions.shortName: In the Matter of Rytr LLC
actions.statusDetail: Decision and Order entered 2024-12-18. Per the FTC's press release, the Commission cited the Administration's AI Executive Order and AI Action Plan, and stated that the complaint failed to satisfy the legal requirements of the FTC Act. The FTC reopened and set aside the order on 2025-12-22. Commission vote 2-0.
actions.outcome: Original order barred selling services dedicated to generating consumer reviews and testimonials; order vacated by FTC set-aside 2025-12-22.
actions.summary: FTC alleged Rytr's AI writing assistant provided the means to generate fake consumer reviews and testimonials. The FTC entered a final order in December 2024 but reopened and set it aside in December 2025.

events.label: FTC reopened and set aside order

factChecks.label: Set-aside
factChecks.value: On 2025-12-22 the FTC issued an order to reopen and set aside the 2024 final consent order involving Rytr LLC.

factChecks.label: Reason
factChecks.value: Per the FTC's press release, the Commission cited the Administration's AI Executive Order and AI Action Plan, and stated that the complaint failed to satisfy the legal requirements of the FTC Act. Commission vote 2-0.

actions.statusDetail: Complaint and proposed stipulated order filed 2024-11-26. FTC filed Motion to Approve 2025-03-11. Case page shows Pending; court approval not yet granted.
actions.outcome: Proposed order would restrict claims about detection accuracy, false alarms, labor-cost savings, and AI-enabled superiority unless substantiated.
actions.summary: FTC alleged Evolv Technologies deceptively advertised that its AI-powered weapons-screening systems could detect all weapons and that AI made them more accurate and cost-effective than traditional metal detectors.

factChecks.label: Status
factChecks.value: Proposed stipulated order; FTC filed Motion to Approve 2025-03-11. Case page last updated 2025-03-11, status Pending.

openItems: Civil action number and current court-approval status need PACER verification.

actions.statusDetail: Proposed order 2024-11-06; final order approved 2025-01-03.
actions.outcome: Bars misrepresentations about ratings and consumer reviews.
actions.summary: FTC charged Sitejabber's AI-enabled review platform with misrepresenting that published ratings and reviews came from consumers who had experienced the product or service, when they had not.

factChecks.label: Final order
factChecks.value: Final order approved 2025-01-03; bars misrepresentations about ratings and consumer reviews.

openItems: FTC matter/file number not verified from public materials.

actions.statusDetail: Complaint and proposed order 2025-01-03; final order approved April 2025.
actions.outcome: $1M payment; substantiation requirement for automated/AI WCAG-compliance claims; endorsement disclosures.
actions.summary: FTC alleged accessiBe made deceptive claims that its automated AI-powered web-accessibility tool could make any website WCAG-compliant and used endorsements without disclosing material connections.

penalties.note: Monetary payment under final order; the cited materials say 'pay $1 million' without characterizing it as a civil penalty or redress.

factChecks.label: Penalty
factChecks.value: accessiBe required to pay $1 million to settle allegations that it misrepresented the ability of its AI-powered tool to make any website WCAG-compliant; final order approved April 2025.

actions.target: Workado LLC f/k/a Content at Scale AI
actions.shortName: In the Matter of Content at Scale AI
actions.statusDetail: Proposed order 2025-04-28; final order 2025-08-28.
actions.outcome: Must substantiate AI-content-detection efficacy claims; retain evidence; notify eligible consumers; compliance reports.
actions.summary: FTC charged Workado with making deceptive and unsubstantiated claims about the accuracy of its AI-content-detection products.

factChecks.label: Final order
factChecks.value: FTC issued final order requiring Workado to stop advertising the accuracy of AI detection products unless it maintains competent and reliable evidence; Docket C-4822, approved 2025-08-28.

actions.target: FBA Machine / Passive Scaling / Bratislav Rozenfeld
actions.statusDetail: Settlement announced as to Rozenfeld/FBA Machine per FTC announcement July 2025; entry status per case page.
actions.outcome: Permanent business-opportunity ban; turnover of financial accounts and real-property proceeds for consumer redress.
actions.summary: FTC alleged defendants falsely guaranteed income through online storefronts using AI-powered software, violating the FTC Act and the Business Opportunity Rule.

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: FTC Matter/File Number X240032; Civil Action Number 2:24-cv-06635-JXN-LDW; FTC alleged defendants falsely guaranteed income through online storefronts using AI-powered software.

actions.target: Ecommerce Empire Builders / Empire Holdings Group / Peter Prusinowski
actions.statusDetail: Complaint announced 2024-09-25; order entered 2025-05-08.
actions.outcome: Business-opportunity ban; asset turnover for refunds.
actions.summary: FTC alleged defendants made deceptive earnings claims about an 'AI-powered Ecommerce Empire' business-opportunity scheme.

factChecks.label: Case record
factChecks.value: FTC case page lists Empire Holdings Group LLC et al. with complaint and order documents.

openItems: Civil action number not verified from FTC public materials; PACER verification pending.

actions.target: Air AI / Air AI Technologies / Caleb Maddix / Ryan O'Donnell / Thomas Lancer
actions.legalAuthority: FTC Act; Telemarketing Sales Rule; Business Opportunity Rule
actions.statusDetail: Complaint filed 2025-08-25. Stipulated final order announced 2026-03-24; $18M judgment largely suspended (inability to pay); $50,000 payment; D. Ariz.; vote 2-0.
actions.outcome: $18M judgment largely suspended; $50,000 payment; business-opportunity ban.
actions.summary: FTC alleged Air AI and its owners used deceptive claims about AI-driven business growth, earnings potential, and refund policies to lure consumers into purchasing business opportunities.

penalties.note: Largely suspended based on inability to pay

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: FTC sued to stop Air AI from using deceptive claims about business growth, earnings potential, and refund policies.

factChecks.label: Settlement
factChecks.value: Stipulated final order announced 2026-03-24; $18M monetary judgment largely suspended based on inability to pay; $50,000 for consumer relief; permanent business-opportunity ban.

actions.statusDetail: Settled administrative order.
actions.outcome: Censure and cease-and-desist; $225,000 civil penalty.
actions.summary: SEC charged Delphia with making false and misleading statements about its use of AI and machine learning in its investment process.
actions.aiIssue: AI washing / false AI investment claims

factChecks.label: Penalty
factChecks.value: SEC settled charges against Delphia and Global Predictions for false and misleading statements about purported AI use; Delphia agreed to a $225,000 civil penalty ($400,000 total across both firms).

actions.shortName: In the Matter of Global Predictions
actions.statusDetail: Settled administrative order.
actions.outcome: Censure and cease-and-desist; $175,000 civil penalty.
actions.summary: SEC charged Global Predictions with falsely claiming to be the 'first regulated AI financial advisor' and making misleading claims about AI-generated forecasts.
actions.aiIssue: AI washing / false AI investment claims

factChecks.label: Penalty
factChecks.value: Global Predictions agreed to a $175,000 civil penalty as part of SEC's first AI-washing enforcement actions.

actions.legalAuthority: Securities Act Section 17(a); Exchange Act Section 10(b), Rule 10b-5
actions.statusDetail: Litigation pending. Parallel DOJ criminal case.
actions.outcome: SEC seeks injunction, penalties, disgorgement/prejudgment interest, officer/director bar.
actions.summary: SEC charged the founder of AI hiring startup Joonko with defrauding investors of at least $21 million by fabricating customers and revenue.

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: SEC v. Ilit Raz, No. 24-civ-4466 (S.D.N.Y.); SEC charged founder of AI recruitment startup Joonko with defrauding investors of at least $21 million.

actions.target: QZ Asset Management / QZ Global / Blake Yeung
actions.statusDetail: SEC litigation ongoing per release.
actions.outcome: SEC seeks permanent injunction, disgorgement, civil penalties.
actions.summary: SEC charged QZ Asset Management and CEO Blake Yeung with falsely claiming QZ Asset would use proprietary AI-based technology to generate extraordinary returns while promising 100% protection for client funds.
actions.aiIssue: AI washing / false AI trading claims

factChecks.label: Allegations
factChecks.value: SEC alleged QZ Asset and Yeung falsely claimed QZ Asset would use proprietary AI-based technology to help generate extraordinary weekly returns while promising 100% protection for client funds.

actions.target: Rimar Capital USA / Rimar Capital LLC / Itai Liptz / Clifford Boro
actions.statusDetail: Settled.
actions.outcome: $310,000 total civil penalties; Liptz bar with right to reapply after five years.
actions.summary: SEC charged Rimar Capital entities and owner Itai Liptz with defrauding investors by making false and misleading statements about purported AI-driven automated trading.
actions.aiIssue: AI washing / false AI trading claims

factChecks.label: Penalties
factChecks.value: Parties agreed to pay $310,000 in total civil penalties. Liptz barred with right to reapply after five years.

openItems: Liptz disgorgement amount of $213,611 reported in the briefing but granularity not independently verified against the administrative order.

actions.shortName: In the Matter of Presto Automation
actions.legalAuthority: Securities Act Section 17(a)(2); Exchange Act Section 13(a); Rules 13a-11, 13a-15(a)
actions.statusDetail: Settled administrative cease-and-desist.
actions.outcome: No civil penalty (cooperation/remediation credit).
actions.summary: SEC found Presto Automation made misleading statements about its flagship AI drive-thru voice product, including the degree to which the product relied on human agents rather than AI.
actions.aiIssue: AI washing / misleading AI product claims

factChecks.label: Order
factChecks.value: Administrative Proceeding File No. 3-22413; SEC found Presto made false and misleading claims about Presto Voice, its AI-assisted drive-thru order-taking product; no civil penalty imposed due to cooperation and remediation.

actions.statusDetail: Litigation pending. Parallel DOJ criminal case.
actions.outcome: SEC alleges over $42 million raised through false AI claims.
actions.summary: SEC charged the founder of Nate, an AI shopping-app startup, with fraudulently raising over $42 million by making false and misleading statements about the company's proprietary AI technology.

allegedHarmUsd.note: Alleged amount raised through false AI claims

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: SEC v. Albert Saniger, No. 1:25-cv-02937 (S.D.N.Y.); SEC charged Nate founder with fraudulently raising over $42 million through false and misleading statements about the company's use of AI.

actions.statusDetail: Settlement announced 2022-06-21. Court oversight ran through 2026-06-27 (four-year term). Formal closeout not publicly announced.
actions.outcome: Meta required to stop Special Ad Audience for housing ads and build a variance-reduction system subject to DOJ/court oversight.
actions.summary: DOJ described the matter as its first Fair Housing Act case challenging algorithmic bias. DOJ alleged Meta's ad-delivery algorithms discriminated on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, disability, familial status, and national origin in the delivery of housing advertisements.

factChecks.label: Settlement
factChecks.value: DOJ described the Meta settlement as the Department's first case challenging algorithmic bias under the Fair Housing Act; Meta agreed to stop Special Ad Audience for housing ads and develop a new system to address disparities.

factChecks.label: Oversight period
factChecks.value: Court oversight and regular compliance review ran through June 27, 2026 per DOJ announcement.

openItems: Formal closeout of the settlement after the 2026-06-27 oversight period has not been publicly announced. Docket verification needed.
Docket number not listed on DOJ press materials; PACER verification pending.

actions.statusDetail: Complaint 2024-08-23; amended complaint 2025-01-07. Only Greystar has a final judgment (2026-03-02). Cortland, RealPage, LivCor, Willow Bridge have proposed final judgments only. Willow Bridge proposed final judgment filed 2026-07-06.
actions.outcome: Injunctive relief and consent decrees sought; no global monetary penalty identified.
actions.summary: DOJ and state attorneys general allege RealPage's algorithmic rental-pricing software enabled coordinated pricing among competing landlords through shared competitively sensitive data.

events.label: Willow Bridge proposed final judgment filed

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: DOJ and state AGs filed a civil antitrust lawsuit against RealPage alleging violations of Sections 1 and 2 of the Sherman Act through algorithmic pricing software.

factChecks.label: Case status
factChecks.value: DOJ case page lists documents through 2026-07-06 including proposed and final judgments for multiple defendants; only Greystar has a final judgment (2026-03-02).

openItems: Docket number not listed on DOJ press materials; PACER verification pending.

actions.statusDetail: Guilty plea 2025-02-13. Sentencing status needs docket verification.
actions.outcome: Pleaded guilty; forfeiture agreement referenced; faces up to five years.
actions.summary: Founder of a purported AI-driven hedge fund pleaded guilty to investment adviser fraud, having falsely promised guaranteed annual returns of 30% or more from AI-driven trading.
actions.aiIssue: AI-company investor fraud / false AI claims

factChecks.label: Guilty plea
factChecks.value: Mina Tadrus pleaded guilty in EDNY Docket No. 23-CR-393 to investment adviser fraud involving false promises that an AI-driven hedge fund would earn guaranteed annual returns of 30% or more.

actions.statusDetail: Indictment unsealed 2024-06-11. Criminal charges pending. Parallel SEC civil action.
actions.outcome: Alleged $27M investment fraud.
actions.summary: DOJ charged the founder of AI hiring startup Joonko with securities fraud and wire fraud for allegedly defrauding investors by misrepresenting the company's customers and revenue.

factChecks.label: Indictment
factChecks.value: SDNY charged Ilit Raz, founder and former CEO of Joonko, with securities fraud and wire fraud for allegedly defrauding investors and misrepresenting customers and revenue of an AI-based hiring company.

actions.statusDetail: Indictment unsealed 2024-09-23; guilty plea 2025-06-26.
actions.outcome: Pleaded guilty to securities fraud and wire fraud.
actions.summary: DOJ charged the founder of AI automation startup SKAEL with defrauding investors by misleading them about the company's revenue and sales.

factChecks.label: Guilty plea
factChecks.value: Baba Nadimpalli, founder and former CEO of SKAEL, pleaded guilty to securities fraud and wire fraud in connection with a scheme to defraud investors.

sources.label: DOJ NDCA guilty plea press release

actions.statusDetail: Indictment announced 2025-04-09. Criminal charges pending. Parallel SEC action.
actions.outcome: Alleged investor fraud.
actions.summary: DOJ charged the former CEO of AI shopping-app Nate with defrauding investors by making false statements about Nate's proprietary AI technology and operational capabilities.

factChecks.label: Indictment
factChecks.value: SDNY charged Albert Saniger, former CEO of Nate, with defrauding investors by making false and misleading statements about Nate's use of proprietary AI technology and operational capabilities.

actions.target: Matthew Derrick Hudson / Invenia Technical Computing
actions.statusDetail: Criminal complaint filed 2025-09-18 and unsealed 2025-09-24; released on bond pending trial. Current status needs docket verification.
actions.outcome: Alleged over $100 million raised from investors through false documents and representations.
actions.summary: DOJ charged the former CEO of a Canadian AI and machine-learning energy-market company with investment fraud for allegedly defrauding U.S. investors through false claims about customers and operations.

allegedHarmUsd.note: Alleged amount raised from investors through false representations

factChecks.label: Complaint
factChecks.value: DOJ alleged former CEO raised over $100 million from investors through false documents and customer representations at an AI/ML energy-market company.

actions.target: Yih-Shyan Liaw / Ruei-Tsang Chang / Ting-Wei Sun
actions.shortName: United States v. Liaw et al.
actions.legalAuthority: Export Control Reform Act conspiracy; smuggling conspiracy; conspiracy to defraud U.S.
actions.statusDetail: Indictment unsealed 2026-03-19. Liaw and Sun arrested; Chang is a fugitive.
actions.outcome: Charges only; alleged diversion of AI servers integrating controlled technology to China.
actions.summary: DOJ charged three individuals with conspiring to unlawfully divert servers integrating controlled U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China, in violation of export controls.

factChecks.label: Indictment
factChecks.value: DOJ announced charges alleging a conspiracy to divert high-performance servers integrating U.S. artificial intelligence technology to China in violation of export controls.

actions.legalAuthority: Continuing financial crimes enterprise; securities fraud; wire fraud conspiracies
actions.statusDetail: Indictment unsealed 2026-04-17.
actions.outcome: Alleged sham contracts and inflated revenue at AI-driven business-automation company.
actions.summary: DOJ charged the former CEO and CFO of NASDAQ-listed AI platform company iLearningEngines with securities fraud and wire fraud conspiracies involving sham contracts and inflated revenue.

factChecks.label: Indictment
factChecks.value: Former CEO and CFO of NASDAQ-listed AI-driven business-automation company indicted for securities fraud, wire fraud conspiracies, and continuing financial crimes enterprise.

actions.target: iTutorGroup Inc. / Tutor Group Ltd. / Shanghai Ping'An Intelligent Education Technology Co.
actions.statusDetail: Five-year consent decree. Settlement announced 2023-09-11.
actions.outcome: $365,000 to applicants; anti-discrimination policy, training, reporting; EEOC monitoring for at least five years.
actions.summary: EEOC alleged that iTutorGroup's tutor application software automatically rejected female applicants over 55 and male applicants over 60, in violation of the ADEA.
actions.aiIssue: Automated hiring discrimination / algorithmic age screening

factChecks.label: Lawsuit
factChecks.value: EEOC filed EEOC v. iTutorGroup, Civil Action No. 1:22-cv-02565 (E.D.N.Y.), alleging tutor application software automatically rejected female applicants over age 55 and male applicants over age 60.

factChecks.label: Settlement
factChecks.value: Consent decree provides $365,000 to applicants and requires anti-discrimination policies, training, injunctions, and EEOC monitoring for at least five years.

actions.legalAuthority: 47 C.F.R. Section 64.6301(a); STIR/SHAKEN caller-ID authentication rules
actions.statusDetail: Consent decree entered 2024-08-21.
actions.outcome: $1,000,000 civil penalty; compliance plan; Know Your Customer / Know Your Upstream Provider controls.
actions.summary: FCC entered a consent decree with Lingo Telecom for STIR/SHAKEN attestation failures tied to the transmission of deepfake AI robocalls impersonating President Biden before the New Hampshire primary.

factChecks.label: Consent decree
factChecks.value: FCC Enforcement Bureau entered a Consent Decree with Lingo Telecom, File No. EB-TCD-24-00036425, requiring a compliance plan and $1,000,000 civil penalty for STIR/SHAKEN attestation violations tied to deepfake AI robocalls.

actions.legalAuthority: Truth in Caller ID Act, 47 U.S.C. Section 227(e); 47 C.F.R. Section 64.1604
actions.statusDetail: Forfeiture order adopted 2024-09-26, released 2024-09-30. FCC referred Kramer to DOJ for collection 2024-12-03.
actions.outcome: $6,000,000 forfeiture.
actions.summary: FCC imposed a $6 million forfeiture against Steve Kramer for illegal robocalls carrying deepfake AI-generated voice messages impersonating President Biden before the New Hampshire primary.
actions.aiIssue: AI deepfake voice / election interference robocalls

factChecks.label: Forfeiture
factChecks.value: FCC imposed a $6,000,000 penalty against Steve Kramer for illegal robocalls carrying a deepfake generative AI voice message imitating President Biden, in violation of the Truth in Caller ID Act.

factChecks.label: Referral
factChecks.value: FCC reported Kramer referral to DOJ for collection on 2024-12-03, with coordination with USTelecom, the Anti-Robocall Multistate Litigation Task Force, and the New Hampshire DOJ.

adjacentMaterials.title: Joint statement on enforcement of automated systems and AI
adjacentMaterials.summary: Four agencies confirmed their view that existing consumer protection, fair lending, civil rights, and employment discrimination laws apply to automated systems and AI-marketed technology, and that use of automation or AI is not an excuse for lawbreaking.

adjacentMaterials.title: Declaratory ruling: AI-generated voices are 'artificial' under the TCPA
adjacentMaterials.summary: FCC declared that calls made with AI-generated voices qualify as 'artificial' under the Telephone Consumer Protection Act, giving the FCC and state attorneys general a clearer enforcement pathway for AI voice-cloning robocall schemes.

adjacentMaterials.title: EEOC amicus brief in Mobley v. Workday (vendor AI hiring liability)
adjacentMaterials.summary: EEOC argued that Workday could be liable as an employment agency, indirect employer, or agent under Title VII, the ADA, and the ADEA where its algorithmic screening tools are used by employers to reject candidates from protected groups.

adjacentMaterials.title: CFPB v. Bank of America (automated fraud filter; not AI-framed)
adjacentMaterials.summary: CFPB found Bank of America relied solely on an automated fraud filter for unemployment-benefit prepaid card investigations, wrongly freezing or blocking accounts. This is an automated-systems / algorithmic-decisioning case, not an AI-branded product enforcement action.

adjacentMaterials.title: CFPB v. Wells Fargo (automated account freezes among broader violations; not AI-framed)
adjacentMaterials.summary: CFPB consent order addressed deposit-account freezes based on automated fraud detection among broader Wells Fargo violations. More than $2 billion in consumer redress and $1.7 billion civil penalty. This is an automated-systems case; the enforcement order does not frame the violations as AI-specific.

changeLog.label: DOJ RealPage
changeLog.change: Willow Bridge proposed final judgment filed, the most recent settlement document in the RealPage algorithmic-pricing case.

changeLog.label: DOJ iLearningEngines
changeLog.change: Former CEO and CFO of NASDAQ-listed AI platform company indicted for securities fraud and wire fraud conspiracies.

changeLog.label: FTC NGL Labs
changeLog.change: Refund claims intake window closed; payments pending distribution.

changeLog.label: FTC Air AI
changeLog.change: Stipulated final order announced; $18M judgment largely suspended; $50,000 payment; business-opportunity ban.

changeLog.label: DOJ AI server diversion
changeLog.change: Three individuals indicted for conspiring to divert AI servers integrating controlled technology to China.

changeLog.label: DOJ RealPage / Greystar
changeLog.change: Greystar final judgment entered, the first final judgment in the RealPage algorithmic-pricing case.

changeLog.label: FTC Rytr
changeLog.change: FTC reopened and set aside the Rytr final order, citing the Administration's AI Executive Order and AI Action Plan, and stating the complaint failed to satisfy FTC Act legal requirements.
